data_in.description: LOTUS flat file containing 484,174 unique 2D structure-organism pairs and 42,166 unique organisms

data_out.description: Table or data frame with organism count per bin: 7,354 organisms with 1 structure, 21,490 with 1–10, 10,683 with 10–100, 374 with >100

domain_knowledge: LOTUS contains 484,174 unique 2D structure-organism pairs spanning 42,166 organisms derived from 31 initial open databases.
Organisms must be grouped into four mutually exclusive bins based on distinct 2D structure count: singleton (1), sparse (2–10), intermediate (11–100), and abundant (>100).
The reported reference counts (7,354 / 21,490 / 10,683 / 374) represent the ground-truth distribution after curation and deduplication across all source databases.
Structure-organism pairs represent documented or inferred associations; a single organism may appear in multiple pairs with different curated structures.

direct_checks: file_exists: verify that a LOTUS flat file (2D structure-organism pairs) is accessible at a public deposit (GitHub release, Zenodo, or documented URL from lotusnprod/lotus-processor)

script_runs: execute a computational script (R, Python, or similar) that loads the flat file, groups unique organisms by cardinality of distinct 2D structures (bins: 1, 1–10, 10–100, >100), and outputs four counts
output_matches_reference: verify that the four computed counts are exactly 7,354 / 21,490 / 10,683 / 374, byte-for-byte, in that order
expert_review: Confirm that the binning logic correctly interprets 'distinct 2D structures per organism' (e.g., handling duplicate or isomeric structures, SMILES normalization, and structure deduplication method) aligns with LOTUS curation standards

[methods] LOTUS flat file containing 484,174 unique 2D structure-organism pairs and 42,166 unique organisms: '484174 (3D|2D) unique referenced structure-organism pairs. They consist of 231330 | 153956 (3D|2D) unique curated structures and 42166 unique organisms'

[methods] Table or data frame with organism count per bin: 7,354 organisms with 1 structure, 21,490 with 1–10, 10,683 with 10–100, 374 with >100: '7354 contain only 1 2D structure, 21490 contain between 1 and 10 2D structures, 10683 contain between 10 and 100 2D structures, 374 contain more than 100 2D structures'

cmd: df <- read.csv('{inputs.lotus_pairs}'); org_counts <- table(df$organism_id); bins <- c(n1 = sum(org_counts == 1), n2_10 = sum(org_counts >= 2 & org_counts <= 10), n11_100 = sum(org_counts >= 11 & org_counts <= 100), n_gt100 = sum(org_counts > 100)); print(bins); stopifnot(bins['n1'] == 7354 && bins['n2_10'] == 21490 && bins['n11_100'] == 10683 && bins['n_gt100'] == 374)

methodology_summary: Load the LOTUS flat file as a data frame with structure ID and organism ID columns.
For each unique organism, count the number of distinct 2D structures associated with it.
Assign each organism to one of four bins: 1 structure, 2–10 structures, 11–100 structures, >100 structures.
Tally the organism count within each bin.
Validation: compare observed bin counts against reported values (7,354, 21,490, 10,683, 374); confirm all four match or report any deviation.

workflow_description: 1. Load the LOTUS flat file (484,174 unique 2D structure-organism pairs across 42,166 organisms) into a data frame. 2. Group organisms by unique 2D structure count and bin into four categories: exactly 1 structure, 1–10 structures, 10–100 structures, and >100 structures. 3. Count the number of organisms in each bin. 4. Validate the observed counts against the reported reference values (7,354, 21,490, 10,683, 374) and report any discrepancies or confirmation.
